label: Kael Orsino
description: A human traveler of early adulthood, this individual bears light skinTone marked by wind-roughened texture and small, permanent nicks along the knuckles. gray eyes sit under straight brows and hold a steady, searching focus characteristic of the newly arrived and purpose-driven. brown hair, worn in a parted style, frames an angular face with a long nose and a firm jawline. Their build remains lean and enduring, defined by long limbs and a carriage shaped by sustained travel rather than settled labor.

info.personality: Restless and goal-oriented, he measures people and places quickly, speaks plainly, and commits decisively once a course is chosen. He seeks hardship as proof of worth, treats new alliances as opportunities for shared advancement, and maintains a disciplined caution without lapsing into suspicion. Purpose, not comfort, governs his decisions, and he prizes clear oaths over elaborate promises.

opts.text: Dawn’s cloudy pall turns the world to bruised violet; wind-driven cinders skitter over scored planks, catching on soot-dark plating, tether rings, and low bulwarks beneath cable-braced rigging and iron-banded spars, while shuttered lantern slits throw thin, stubborn light. Near the helm, the lean helmsman keeps the Cinderwake steady with a quiet, braced patience, and the scar-browed captain stands on deck overseeing the ship’s readiness for the electrostatic gales—nothing else on this sweep looks unusual, and no new way off the deck presents itself.

Dawn’s clouded light lies dull on the soot-dark plating; tether rings stud the cinder-scoured deck, low bulwarks hem the edge, and braced rigging and cable-stays hum faintly with a prickling electrostatic charge, shuttered lantern housings giving only a grudging gray. Near the helm the lean helmsman keeps the ship steady with both hands, the broad-shouldered captain stands watchful a few paces off like a verdict, and a young traveler lingers by the lash points and hatch coamings, eyes roaming as if measuring who belongs.

Cloudy dawn leaks a flat gray light over ash-scored planking, tether rings, low bulwarks, soot-dark plating, shuttered lanterns, and cable-braced rigging on iron-banded spars; beyond the rail, violet storm-glare stains the horizon like a bruise that won’t confess. Two young travelers linger near the weathered rail, the helmsman stands braced at the wheel like it owes him money, and the captain paces with the proprietorial impatience of a man supervising the concept of safety; from here no obvious gangplank or easy exit shows, and everything looks cinched down for hard weather.

opts.label: ⚔ Perform a complex dance on the deck.

opts.text: Flat gray dawn watches in judgment as the ship rolls underfoot; the routine threads between tether rings and bulwark with irritating competence, ending cleanly without so much as a toe to the wrong plank. The two young travelers blink, then grin at each other like they’ve just been handed a free scandal, while the helmsman flicks a quick approving glance and keeps the wheel like it’s a religious obligation; the captain’s pacing stutters, offended that grace has occurred without permits.
Well. That’s one way to keep warm without paying for rum.
Good footwork. Don’t do it near the lines.
If the storm takes us, at least we’ll die cultured.
Marvelous. Now, unless that dance is rated for rigging maintenance, kindly keep your heroics where they don’t tempt the sea to notice us.

Clear afternoon light washes the deck in hard contrast, picking out soot-dark plating, tether rings bolted along the low bulwarks, shuttered lantern housings, and rigging cinched tight with iron-banded spars built to survive ashfall and electrostatic gales; a dry wind carries faint grit across the boards. The captain stands with an overseeing stillness near middeck, the helmsman keeps close to the helm with hands and eyes on the ship’s set, while the two young travelers linger nearby as passengers—watching the lines, the horizon, and each other in wary, quiet measure.
You travel like you expect trouble, same as me. What brings you onto a ship that sails when routes die—profit, penance, or duty?
Clouds press low over the soot-dark deck as the evening chill creeps in; Kestrel Arvant shifts his weight with a traveler’s readiness and studies you like he’s weighing the cost of honesty.

You’re not wrong—trouble has a way of finding the roads I take. Duty, mostly: I’m carrying papers that don’t survive a closed checkpoint, and the Cinderwake still sails when the land routes choke out.

journal.content:
You stand on the Cinderwake’s soot-armored deck, boots grinding cinder into the scored planks as violet storm-glare leaks through shuttered lantern slits. The rigging hums with electrostatic wind and prayer-chains alike, and you keep your balance by habit, a sanctioned passenger under Imperial charter in the dark half of the Imperium where every voyage is a penance.

Ashfall clings to your sleeves; crew and soldiers move with the tight, watchful discipline of people who know the void-lanes and the skies both can turn hungry without warning. You speak plainly when spoken to, keep your oaths close, and let the ship’s hard weather feel like proof that you belong here.

facts.subject: Current Situation
facts.fact: This vessel is hardened for ashfall and electrostatic gales; unsecured gear and careless bodies are taken by the wind.

facts.subject: Current Situation
facts.fact: In Imperium Nihilus, orders arrive late, garbled, or not at all, so discipline and written oaths carry more weight than promises.

facts.subject: Current Situation
facts.fact: You are here as a sanctioned, mission-bound operative under Imperial authority, expected to obey chain of command and keep faith outwardly unbroken.

plan.label: Ash Oath, Rift Whisper
plan.description: You wake beneath a sky that never truly brightens, where violet storm-glare turns the ashfall into a slow, suffocating blizzard. The Cinderwake creaks like a penitent in chains, its deck-scars fresh with the grit of another night survived under rift-shadow. A message arrives in the ugliest way the Imperium delivers truth: sealed, blood-warm, and already surrounded by lies. Whatever waits at Ashshelf Dock is not merely late on its tithe—it is deciding what it must become to endure.
plan.plot: Opening hook: a void-waxed Sealed Letter is found lashed into the rigging inside a dead courier’s prayer-cloth, untouched by the storm as if the ash refuses it. The letter bears an Inquisitorial transit cipher and commands the bearer to locate a silent astropathic node on Vespera Tertius before it is “answered” by the warp. Severin Drach pushes to keep the ship moving and the crew ignorant, while Severan Dusk quietly warns Kael Orsino that someone aboard has already begun making offerings to a machine that should not be worshipped. At Ashshelf Dock, Corvin Rake trades names for ammunition and points the party toward a sealed prefab—Aurorashade Hab—where a forbidden beacon hums beneath Ecclesiarchal hymns played backward. The antagonist, an ash-smeared cult leader hiding behind sanctioned rites, is using a tainted Iron Amulet and a Mechanicus-stamped Bronze Medallion to tune the beacon and “steal” a clear signal through the Great Rift’s interference. The twist: the beacon is not simply calling for rescue—it is broadcasting a location-rite that will guide a warp entity to the dock and the Cinderwake alike, and one of the ship’s officers has been feeding it precise course and tide timings. If the players purge the beacon they win only a local reprieve; if they fail, the dock becomes a lantern for nightmares, and the Imperium’s silence gains a voice.

characters.description: A compact, ship-hardened commander with bronze skin and a long healed cleft over the left brow; his broken-set nose and heavy shoulders make him look built for boarding actions. He speaks in clipped orders and watches the crew like a man counting rations in a famine.
characters.publicFace: A loyal shipboard captain keeping the Cinderwake alive through ash-storms and dockside treachery.
characters.hiddenTruth: He is hiding evidence that the ship’s route and schedules have been leaked to someone at Ashshelf Dock, and he will sacrifice outsiders to keep his command intact.
characters.locationHint: On the Cinderwake’s weather deck and near any navigational charts or cargo tallies.
characters.name: Severin Drach

characters.description: Lean and long-limbed, with wind-chapped cheeks and callused palms; his brown eyes rarely stop moving, measuring ropes, people, and exits. He is careful with faith—he recites the litanies, but never loudly.
characters.publicFace: A competent helmsman who knows the storms and keeps his head down.
characters.hiddenTruth: He has noticed ritualized “maintenance” on a piece of equipment that should not exist on a simple ash-sailer, and he is terrified it’s attracting something through the Rift.
characters.locationHint: Near the wheel, rigging stations, and wherever the crew gathers to whisper under storm-noise.
characters.name: Severan Dusk

characters.description: A narrow-faced man with stained cuffs and a canteen-worker’s habitual smile that never reaches the eyes; his hands shake only when he counts. He speaks in low, practiced tones, as if every word has been priced.
characters.publicFace: A dockside barkeep who hears everything and sells only what keeps him breathing.
characters.hiddenTruth: He has been paid to misdirect anyone asking about the silent astropaths, but he is keeping one true lead in reserve as insurance.
characters.locationHint: Ashshelf Dock’s drinking corner and supply queues, where desperate laborers trade rumors for scrip.
characters.name: Corvin Rake

characters.description: A robed figure crusted with ash-salt, face hidden behind a soot-blackened devotional mask studded with scavenged vox-grilles. Their voice carries the cadence of sermons, but the words feel like hooks behind the teeth.
characters.publicFace: A strict lay-preacher coordinating ‘purity rites’ for dockworkers and the sick.
characters.hiddenTruth: A Chaos-corrupted cult conductor using a tuned beacon-rite to cut through the Rift’s interference and draw a warp presence to the dock—then weaponize the panic and ‘miracle’ that follows.
characters.locationHint: Aurorashade Hab and any sealed rooms where power cables, incense, and forbidden machinery intersect.
characters.matchHint: ash-masked lay-preacher leading forbidden beacon rites

places.storyRole: Primary contact point: rumors, bribes, and the first proof that something is harvesting the dock’s fear.
Investigation hub: evidence of illicit Mechanicus-linked parts and where the Bronze Medallion’s chain of custody can be traced.
Dungeon and ritual site: the silent astropathic node has been repurposed into a beacon-rite chamber that must be purged or destroyed.

items.locationHint: Lodged in the Cinderwake’s rigging within a dead courier’s prayer-cloth; recovered during the first storm-watch.
Hidden among ‘misplaced’ crates and sealant tins in Ashbound Stores, bearing faint Mechanicus stamping.
Worn by a rite-leader or kept near the beacon apparatus inside Aurorashade Hab, warm despite the cold ash-air.

essentialInformation: The player learns an official sealed dispatch has reached the Cinderwake under impossible circumstances.
The player learns the dispatch concerns a missing or silent astropathic node on Vespera Tertius.
The player learns the message implies immediate danger if the node is not secured quickly.
The player learns Ashshelf Dock is being controlled through ‘purity rites’ that double as intimidation and recruitment.
The player learns there is a sealed site (Aurorashade Hab) tied to the silence and recent disappearances.
The player learns someone aboard ship (or tied to its schedule) is being used to time the rites.
The player learns a Mechanicus-marked component (the Bronze Medallion) is being used to tune or authorize the beacon machinery.
The player learns the Iron Amulet is not a simple charm but a conduit linked to the rite’s escalation.
The player learns the beacon is broadcasting a ‘guidance’ signal meant to draw something in, not send a plea out.
The player learns the rite has a near-term completion window and delaying will make the breach harder to stop.
The player learns stopping the rite requires either purging the tuning focus or physically destroying the beacon apparatus.

turningPoints.involvedCharacter: ash-masked lay-preacher leading forbidden beacon rites
essentialInformation: The player learns the beacon’s ‘answer’ is imminent and must be severed now.
The player learns that removing or sanctifying the tuning focus can collapse the rite without saving everyone involved.
The player learns that any outcome will be reframed by Imperial authorities as either miracle or heresy depending on who controls the report.
The player learns the surviving evidence will trigger competing claims (Ecclesiarchy, Mechanicus, and Inquisition) and future reprisals.

possibleFlags.triggerDescription: Set when the player obtains the Sealed Letter.
Set when the player opens/reads the Sealed Letter or otherwise uses it to extract its contents.
Set when the player obtains the Bronze Medallion.
Set when the player uses the Bronze Medallion to access, tune, or prove authorization regarding the beacon machinery.
Set when the player obtains the Iron Amulet.
Set when the player uses the Iron Amulet (intentionally or accidentally), triggering or interacting with warp-tainted effects.
Set when the player successfully gains actionable information at Ashshelf Dock (e.g., through Corvin Rake or overheard leads).
Set when the player searches Ashbound Stores and uncovers signs of illicit Mechanicus-linked supply diversion.
Set when the player finds the true location/entry method for Aurorashade Hab.
Set when the player confirms the ‘silent node’ is being used as part of a beacon-rite.
Set when the player gathers enough proof to confront or report Severin Drach’s concealment or complicity.
Set when the player resolves Severin Drach’s fate (arrested, executed, confined, or coerced into compliance).
Set when the player successfully collapses the rite and purges/cripples the beacon apparatus.
Set when the rite completes or the beacon remains active long enough to draw in a warp presence.

goals.description: Recover the dispatch that reached the Cinderwake and confirm its legitimacy before it ‘disappears’ into shipboard routine.
Read the dispatch and establish a clear destination and priority—then make landfall contacts without drawing a purge-mob.
Trace the silence to its source: locate Aurorashade Hab and identify how the astropathic node is being exploited.
End the beacon-rite—by purge, sabotage, or sanctioned destruction—before it becomes a lighthouse for the warp.
Decide what to do with Severin Drach once you have proof: expose him, bind him to obedience, or remove him as a liability.

endingCards.title: A Local Victory
endingCards.text: The beacon dies with a sound like a held breath released; the dock survives the night, and the ashfall resumes its ordinary cruelty. The silence beyond the Rift remains, but it does not answer here—today.

endingCards.title: A Lantern for the Warp
endingCards.text: Something notices Ashshelf Dock, and the air becomes crowded with meanings the human mind was not built to hold. The Imperium will call what follows a disaster, a purge, or a miracle—depending on who signs the report.

endingCards.title: Chain of Command Rewritten
endingCards.text: Severin Drach’s fate becomes a lesson the crew will repeat in whispers: survival has rules, and rules have teeth. The Cinderwake sails on, but its loyalties no longer feel like simple rope and iron.

endingCards.title: Orders Obeyed, Eyes Opened
endingCards.text: You have read the words that were never meant to be spoken aloud, and the ink feels like a contamination. Whatever authority issued them now owns a piece of your future.

endingCards.title: Innocence Proves Nothing
endingCards.text: In Imperium Nihilus, every problem is also a jurisdictional dispute, and every solution leaves evidence. The war continues, and the darkness keeps its accounts.

info.startingNarrative:
You stand on the Cinderwake’s soot-armored deck, boots grinding cinder into the scored planks as violet storm-glare leaks through shuttered lantern slits. The rigging hums with electrostatic wind and prayer-chains alike, and you keep your balance by habit, a sanctioned passenger under Imperial charter in the dark half of the Imperium where every voyage is a penance.

Ashfall clings to your sleeves; crew and soldiers move with the tight, watchful discipline of people who know the void-lanes and the skies both can turn hungry without warning. You speak plainly when spoken to, keep your oaths close, and let the ship’s hard weather feel like proof that you belong here.
info.startingCharacterState: Restless, focused, and steady—alert to danger without giving in to paranoia.

relationships.context: Both are currently in PLACE_the_cinderwake, making a basic shipboard/port acquaintance likely for a newly arrived traveler.
Both are currently in PLACE_the_cinderwake; Kael’s arrival and goal-oriented nature could plausibly bring him into brief contact with the vessel’s commander.
If Kael takes passage, signs on, or otherwise falls under ship authority in PLACE_the_cinderwake, Severin Drach would be the clear commanding superior.
